questions.text: I find that I can help people feel heard and valued within a group.
I practice important skills repeatedly until they become second nature.
I feel a responsibility to contribute more than my fair share.
I often find out about interpersonal tensions after they have already escalated.
I wake from nightmares with my heart pounding and the fear persisting long after I'm fully awake.
I believe that too much planning can be just as harmful as too little.
I am inclined to avoid exercising authority if it would create tension.
I believe that the felt weight of suffering is what keeps moral reasoning honest and grounded.
I feel responsible for making sure the right things happen.
I find that I can discuss tragic events with analytical clarity without feeling personally implicated.
I believe that every interaction contains far more information than the words exchanged.
I tend to be unaware of the relational dynamics underlying a group discussion.
I feel that an adaptable person is a valuable person.
I feel that ensuring my own financial security comes before generosity.
I hold the view that my emotional steadiness allows me to be a grounding presence.
I tend to see my career advancement as one of my highest priorities.
I rarely experience the kind of emotional flooding that makes it hard to think.
I find that I can assess the severity and nature of someone's difficult situation without my emotional state being influenced by their distress.
